name: Voter Action Project
subject: The attacks aren't stopping
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Whether it's these 27 different state bills, folks, or the SAVE Act in Congress, it's abundantly clear that opponents of voting rights are only ramping up their attacks on our fundamental rights . We have to put pressure on them to stop at both the state and federal level.
RUSH $25 or more right now to stand with us and fight to defend our right to vote wherever it's under attack >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=100525_3&amount=25]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
BCJ: State Bills Would Require Passport or Birth Certificate to Register to Vote [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=100525_3&amount=25]
Team,
We've told you that voter I.D. laws are unfairly biased against people of color, young people, and unmarried women. However, across the country, states are introducing bills to make these laws even MORE restrictive at an alarming rate.
The nonpartisan Brennan Center for Justice has the details: “ Lawmakers in at least 27 states have introduced...bills in 2025 that would require some or all voters to show citizenship documents to vote .” Not just when you register, but every single time you show up to cast a ballot.
While citizenship is required to vote, rigorous research shows time and again that instances of votes cast by non-citizens are extremely rare. At least 21 million citizens, however, do not have immediate access to the I.D. documents these laws would demand, including 3.1 million voters who lack documents entirely.
The suppressive SAVE Act is stalled in Congress. So opponents of voting rights have turned to the states. These bills aren’t motivated by a desire for free and fair elections – they’re just another voter suppression tactic.
We need your help to defend the right to vote in these 27 states and beyond. Will you chip in with $25 or whatever you can today to help us fight back against voter suppression and intimidation? >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=100525_3&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=100525_3&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=100525_3&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=100525_3&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=100525_3&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=100525_3&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=100525_3&amount=35]
The problem is not only that these bills would block millions of Americans from voting, but also that these requirements to vote are a massive burden for election officials and many voters alike.
These intimidating requirements also come alongside other suppressive policies stocked into these bills: Mandatory voter purges, reduced early voting, closing polling places, restrictions on mail-in ballots, and so much more.
We need to speak with one loud, clear voice and tell every state legislature: We won't stand for any of these suppressive policies. Not now, not ever.
Opponents of voting rights aren't going to stop their work to restrict voting until we make them stop. Fuel our efforts to resist $25 or whatever you can today >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=100525_3&amount=25]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=100525_3&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=100525_3&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.

P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States
This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. name: GOTV PAC
subject: Even Fox News agrees with us??
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GET OUT THE VOTE PAC NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR ANY CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email09042025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email09042025&amount=25]
Peter ,
You know things are bad for Trump when even Fox News is saying it out loud.
Their headline this week: “Trump’s shadow looms large over heated races one month before Election Day.”
They’re admitting what we've been saying: If Trump’s allies lose in New Jersey, Virginia, and Pennsylvania next month, it’ll be a clear sign that voters are rejecting his chaos, his shutdown, and his second-term agenda.
That’s right: the 2025 elections are shaping up to be an early referendum on Trump’s presidency.
If Democrats win big, it’ll prove voters are fed up with the dysfunction, the cruelty, and the endless shutdowns, and it’ll send shockwaves through Washington.
GOTV PAC is working in all three states to turn out Democratic voters — but we need your help to make sure Trump and his allies get the message loud and clear. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email10052025]
If Trump loses next month, he loses the narrative. Let’s make it happen:
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

Our professional phone bankers specialize in long- and short-term GOTV campaigns, event registration, voting assistance in heavily gerrymandered states, fundraising, and call time management. Our mission is to flip red districts blue, any elected position from dog walker to Congress member.
Learn more at: gotv.vote [https://www.gotv.vote/]

Peter – Have you seen this worrying update?
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116682568/574095654/-1473124489?amount=25&refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-10-05_J6C_FBN_ES-32641&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82OTYwNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YjgxNzVkNS00OGEyLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=voBnoQTQUsS2a6_DyQONsyxyln6YQuK5GoHx7ibO-fM=)
---
| | You read that right, Peter. Republicans are openly using the new January 6th subcommittee to shift the blame for the insurrection away from Donald Trump. When Zoe Lofgren served on the original January 6th committee, she and her committee members worked across party lines to reveal the truth about the tragic events of the day, and Trump’s role in inciting them. But the Republicans leading this new subcommittee just made it clear that they’ll stop at nothing to unravel the work that Zoe and her colleagues did to protect Trump and his far-right allies. **[Zoe Lofgren is committed to preserving the truth about January 6th and finally holding Donald Trump accountable, but she needs your support to keep up the fight in Congress. We understand that the Republicans’ shutdown puts many in a challenging position, but if you’re able, will you chip in $10 or anything you can to fuel Zoe’s fight against Trump? >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116682569/574095682/-1473124489?amount=10&refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-10-05_J6C_FBN_ES-32641&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82OTYwNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YjgxNzVkNS00OGEyLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=voBnoQTQUsS2a6_DyQONsyxyln6YQuK5GoHx7ibO-fM=)**
---
[ **CHIP IN NOW >>** ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116682570/574095683/-1473124489?amount=25&refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-10-05_J6C_FBN_ES-32641&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82OTYwNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YjgxNzVkNS00OGEyLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=voBnoQTQUsS2a6_DyQONsyxyln6YQuK5GoHx7ibO-fM=)
---
Peter, Donald Trump’s disinformation was dangerous for our democracy then, and it’s only getting worse now. His “big lie” that the 2020 election was stolen led to violent riots by his supporters on January 6th, and now he’s using his second term to suppress voters and spread conspiracy theories about the 2026 midterms. **Telling the truth about the events of January 6th is crucial to ensuring that every person involved is held responsible, the events of that terrible day never repeat, and our democracy remains intact. ** **[That’s why Zoe Lofgren is fighting to preserve the truth about January 6th and stand up to those who are trying to whitewash history. If you’re able, will you support her work by donating any amount today? >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116682571/574095684/-1473124489?amount=25&refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-10-05_J6C_FBN_ES-32641&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82OTYwNiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YjgxNzVkNS00OGEyLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=voBnoQTQUsS2a6_DyQONsyxyln6YQuK5GoHx7ibO-fM=)**
---

clean_body: | | | = | | | Peter – Have you seen this worrying update? = | | You read that right, Peter. Republicans are openly using the new January 6th subcommittee to shift the blame for the insurrection away from Donald Trump. When Zoe Lofgren served on the original January 6th committee, she and her committee members worked across party lines to reveal the truth about the tragic events of the day, and Trump’s role in inciting them. But the Republicans leading this new subcommittee just made it clear that they’ll stop at nothing to unravel the work that Zoe and her colleagues did to protect Trump and his far-right allies. = = Peter, Donald Trump’s disinformation was dangerous for our democracy then, and it’s only getting worse now. His “big lie” that the 2020 election was stolen led to violent riots by his supporters on January 6th, and now he’s using his second term to suppress voters and spread conspiracy theories about the 2026 midterms. Telling the truth about the events of January 6th is crucial to ensuring that every person involved is held responsible, the events of that terrible day never repeat, and our democracy remains intact. = = In solidarity,Team Lofgren | | Congresswoman Zoe Lofgren is a strong fighter for California’s values in Washington. As the Chair of the California Democratic Congressional Delegation, she has been on the front lines of expanding and protecting the Democratic Majority. Zoe has dedicated her career to fighting for our diverse communities and needs your help. | | | Paid for by Lofgren for Congress Lofgren for Congress PO Box 913 San Jose, CA 95113 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive emails from us, please .

This fundraising deadline is our next opportunity to show everyone how powerful our movement is. That we can root out corruption and make sure every dollar in our state budget is spent serving people, not corporations. That we can ensure everyone in Michigan can afford to live here and thrive.
This is our chance to beat my opponents’ billionaire money and fight for the future we want — one where we build an economy that works for everyone.

name: Sara Jacobs
subject: Why Speaker Johnson is refusing to swear in Adelita S. Grijalva....
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-grijalva [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-grijalva]
Hey friend, Last Tuesday, the people of Arizona’s 7th District overwhelmingly voted to send Adelita S. Grijalva to Congress. In the special election, she won by nearly 40 points, filling the seat left vacant after the passing of her father, Congressman Raúl M. Grijalva, in March.
Adelita came to Washington this week ready to be sworn in and get to work. But Speaker Mike Johnson refused — he shut down the House and blocked her from taking the oath of office.
There’s no rule stopping new members from being sworn in when the House isn’t in session (Johnson’s excuse) or during a shutdown — in fact, there’s recent precedent set by Republicans who very conveniently swore in members of their own party this year, just hours after being elected in special elections. This isn’t about procedure. It’s about power — and it’s a cover-up.
Yes, I’m talking about the Epstein files.
Here’s the truth: once Adelita is sworn in, Democrats (and some Republicans) will have the 218 signatures needed to force the release of the Epstein files — documents detailing a billion-dollar sex trafficking operation that Donald Trump and his allies desperately want to keep hidden.
Adelita has been clear from day one: she’ll be the deciding vote to release those files. That’s exactly why Johnson is stopping her.
Over the summer, Johnson shut down the House to avoid a vote on the Epstein files. And now he’s denying representation to more than 750,000 Arizonans to protect Donald Trump, and to keep the American people from knowing the truth.
Right now, Adelita and I are doing everything we can to fight back — but we need your help. Can you split a donation between our campaigns today so we can expose this corruption and flip the House next year? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-grijalva]
If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-grijalva?express_lane=true&amount=25]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-grijalva?express_lane=true&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-grijalva?express_lane=true&amount=100]
DONATE OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em-grijalva]
This isn’t just an attack on Congresswoman-elect Grijalva, or her constituents — it’s an attack on democracy, accountability, and your right to know the truth about your elected officials.
Thank you for standing with us — more soon.
—Sara

name: Rob Bonta
subject: NEW: Lawsuit against Trump and Hegseth
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-25]
Friends, critical update for you tonight — I’ve filed a lawsuit with Oregon Attorney General Dan Rayfield and the City of Portland to block the Trump administration from deploying federalized California National Guard troops to Oregon.
Here’s how we got here:
This week, Trump and Hegseth federalized the Oregon National Guard and deployed them to Portland, using the same unlawful scheme he used to federalize California’s National Guard earlier this year.
Yesterday, a federal district court issued an order blocking the administration from doing that — but instead of respecting that order, Trump is trying to circumvent it by unlawfully deploying federalized California National Guard troops to Oregon.
Now, we’re fighting back in court — because Trump is not king, and California’s National Guard is not Trump’s “Royal Guard.”
If you’re with me, I need you to raise your voice. Sign on to support our fight against Trump’s unlawful deployment of military troops for civilian law enforcement. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/JmKTmAvWe0WTNlVBHYLq4Q2]
Let’s be really clear about what’s happening here: The President is seeking to use federalized California National Guard troops as federal military police — anywhere in the country, whenever he wants.
First it was LA. Today, it’s Portland. Tomorrow, it could be any other city in the United States.
And we’ve already seen the consequences of these unlawful deployments firsthand: trust between communities and law enforcement breaks down, local economies suffer, and public safety is undermined.
I refuse to stand idly by while Trump and Hegseth turn our cities into their military training grounds, weaponize our troops for their political gains, and blatantly and repeatedly abuse their power.
Add your name if you agree: Our cities are not training grounds, and our troops aren’t political pawns. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/JmKTmAvWe0WTNlVBHYLq4Q2]
ADD YOUR NAME [https://secure.ngpvan.com/JmKTmAvWe0WTNlVBHYLq4Q2]
Thank you for standing with us — and with Oregon — in this fight.
Updates to come.
—Rob

Rob Bonta

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 10/5/2025
body:
Colorado's conservative youth are on the rise
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**October 5, 2025** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST**
| | Dear Readers,
Across Colorado, something powerful is stirring.
From Morgan County to Mesa County, young conservatives are stepping off the sidelines and into the fight for their state’s future.
At the [Morgan County GOP’s annual Candidate Night](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), the message was clear: **Colorado’s youth are no longer waiting to be asked—they’re showing up, speaking up, and leading.** Representative Dusty Johnson, the state’s youngest legislator, summed it up best:
_“It takes both the mature, seasoned voices and the younger energy in the system to make it all work. That’s how we build a balanced and healthy future together.”_
And she’s not alone. Among the Republican caucus are six young legislators—Caldwell, Barron, Gonzalez, Johnson, Weinberg, and Soper—some already serving as House Whip and Minority Leader. In Washington, Lauren Boebert (CD-4) and Gabe Evans (CD-8) bring that same youthful energy to Congress. Add two young State Board of Education members and a CU Regent, and it’s clear: Colorado’s future is being shaped by conviction, not complacency.
For a bit of history—Colorado’s youngest known legislator was Thomas W. Nevin, elected in 1934 at just 24 years, 8 months, and 26 days old—technically younger than the constitutional minimum at the time. Former Rep. Jerry Kopel and the Secretary of State’s archives confirmed Nevin’s early election, proving that **youthful leadership in Colorado politics is nothing new—it’s part of our state’s DNA.**
These young leaders aren’t figureheads. They’re writing bills, fighting red tape, defending parental rights, and standing up for freedom. Groups like the [Colorado Young Republicans](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), [Turning Point USA](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and [American Conservation Coalition](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) are fueling that same fire at the grassroots—connecting new voters, training future leaders, and rebuilding from the ground up.
Meanwhile, we've had an influx of young people submitting commentaries here at Rocky Mountain Voice. **Two of this week's submissions among our most read:**
[Young conservative writer Austin DeWitt](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) has been unafraid to take on the establishment. His reporting from Mesa County exposed how the National Education Association’s 2025 handbook prioritized ideology over education—until it was quietly deleted from public view. And [Sean Bateman](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) argued that the true war we face today isn’t fought with bullets, but with ideas—and across Colorado, more people are waking up and choosing to fight for truth.
Colorado’s youth aren’t buying into cynicism or apathy. They’re reclaiming their state—with truth, courage, and conviction.
This movement isn’t just political. It’s spiritual. It’s cultural. It’s generational.
Because when the next generation stands up—mountains move.
In liberty and truth,
Team RMV
---
| | |
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[The dangerous gentleman enters the race: Victor Marx launches gubernatorial bid](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The crowd inside Phil Long Music Hall stood and cheered as Victor Marx made it official. On Oct. 1, the Marine veteran and founder of All Things Possible Ministries announced his run for Colorado governor, joined by local pastors, veterans and elected officials—backing his call to restore justice and public safety.

**[VSSA teacher apologizes—debate persists over classroom remarks about Charlie Kirk and Trump](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) A storm continues to brew at Vail Ski and Snowboard Academy (VSSA) after a longtime teacher’s controversial remarks in class. The comments came shortly after conservative activist Charlie Kirk was assassinated, setting off heated debate in the community about free speech, professional boundaries, and accountability in schools.

**[Durango 9-R’s Monday update comes as parents dispute the “misinformation” label](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Durango School District 9-R holds its State of the District tonight at the Impact Career Innovation Center. The district’s event page bills it as a community update with test-score dashboards and a Q&A. The Durango Herald said leaders plan to confront “misinformation.” Parent Jason Mietchen hears it differently: “We’ve had to counteract the misinformation for years. The school puts out a ton of it.”

|
---
**STATE**
**[TABOR Didn’t Build the Potholes](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY MICHAEL HANCOCK, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Colorado’s roads crumble, our classrooms overflow, and every budget cycle we hear the same refrain: “It’s TABOR’s fault.” Critics of the Taxpayer’s Bill of Rights insist that this constitutional guardrail has starved government of the resources it needs to educate children, maintain infrastructure, and keep the state running. But what if the potholes and crowded classrooms aren’t a symptom of too little revenue, but of misplaced priorities?_
__
**[USDA move to Fort Collins could add 6,000 jobs and $1B in output, study finds](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[COLORADO POLITICS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The U.S. Department of Agriculture’s planned relocation of up to 2,600 employees to Fort Collins could bring more than $1 billion in new business output and over 6,000 new jobs to the area by the end of next year, according to a study by the think tank Common Sense Institute.
__
**[When will the health-related employment bubble burst?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY MIKE O'DONNELL, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _A few weeks ago, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announced that it had inadvertently overstated—by more than 100%—the number of new jobs created across the United States during the last full year of the Biden/Harris Administration.
_
_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[Western Cattlemen Push Back on Federal Land Restrictions](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE FENCE POST](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Several groups of cattle producers could be seen walking the halls of Congress and the U.S. Department of Agriculture in recent weeks. No, they weren’t lost. They were detailing some of the ways government prevents cattle producers from being profitable and identifying solutions that could help. __
**[Millions donated to Obama Foundation funneled to Tides for anti-Israel activism at Ivy League colleges](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[NEW YORK POST](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Millions of dollars sent to Barack Obama’s foundation has ended up being donated to a progressive fund that supported anti-Israel groups, accused of setting up encampments at Ivy League universities, according to public documents.
__
**[Congress Gridlock Forces Government Shutdown as Spending Talks Collapse](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[FOX NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The federal government is officially entering a partial shutdown on Wednesday after the midnight funding deadline passed with Democrats and Republicans failing to agree on a funding bill.

**[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[Denver’s Spending Grew 60% in a Decade, But Revenues and Population Lagged Far Behind](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Denver’s budget has grown sharply over the past ten years, far faster than its population and its ability to bring in tax dollars. A new analysis from the Common Sense Institute shows inflation-adjusted spending per resident rose about 60 percent since 2015.

**[Why the Douglas County School Board Election on November 4th Matters More Than Ever](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY ANDY JONES, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _As the leaves turn in Douglas County, so does the page to another pivotal moment in our community’s story: the school board election on November 4, 2025. For parents juggling carpools, teachers fine-tuning lesson plans, and students eyeing college applications, this vote might feel like just another item on a crowded ballot._

**[“They don’t care”: Unleashed podcast spotlights Durango parents’ loss of trust](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Hunter Opilla didn’t expect to speak at a school board meeting when his family moved to Durango two years ago. But after learning about the district’s gender bathroom policy—and the board’s decision to reverse a superintendent directive—he says he felt he had no choice.

**_[READ MORE LOCAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_**
|
---
**COMMENTARY**
**[When Justice Becomes Partisan, Freedom Dies](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY C. J. GARBO,[ ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Picture, for a moment, a reversal of roles. Suppose a radical on the right assassinated one of the most visible voices on the left in front of live cameras. Imagine if, after that horror, crowds of conservatives cheered, called for more, and excused the violence as justified._ __
**[Beyond the rhetoric: Schools, unions, and the battle for objective truth in education](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY LAUREEN BOLL,[ ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _DCSD recently voted in favor of requiring parental consent, or “opt-in,” for students to participate in the upcoming Healthy Kids Colorado Survey, also known as HKCS. HKCS is an anonymous survey that is offered to all school districts in the state every-other-year, and much of the information that’s collected from middle and high school students is deeply personal, invasive, and has nothing to do with academics._

**[When did the Upper and Lower Basin of the Colorado River become a thing?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY STEVE HARRIS,[ ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Though I was not in attendance in 1922 (don’t laugh), the minutes of the negotiations are very thorough. At the first Commission meeting starting on January 30, 1922, the negotiations were centered on separating the Colorado River flow by the potential for irrigated lands in each state._

**[The Sword of the Spirit – Transformative Power](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY DRAKE HUNTER,[ ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _When I was younger, I was fascinated with Disneyland in Anaheim, California. My dad had worked there, and my mom had books on Walt Disney—how he dreamed up new worlds, turned them into concepts, and then made them a reality._
__
**[READ MORE COMMENTARY](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|

|
---
**🚴♀️ DID YOU KNOW?** **Queen Victoria made the tricycle a royal trend.** In 1881, while touring the Isle of Wight, Queen Victoria noticed a woman riding a tricycle and was instantly fascinated. She ordered two for herself and even requested that the inventor, James Starley, deliver them personally. Once the queen was seen riding, tricycles quickly became a must-have among Britain’s elite.
|

clean_body: Colorado's conservative youth are on the rise | | | | October 5, 2025 SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST | | Dear Readers, Across Colorado, something powerful is stirring. From Morgan County to Mesa County, young conservatives are stepping off the sidelines and into the fight for their state’s future. At the , the message was clear: Colorado’s youth are no longer waiting to be asked—they’re showing up, speaking up, and leading. Representative Dusty Johnson, the state’s youngest legislator, summed it up best: “It takes both the mature, seasoned voices and the younger energy in the system to make it all work. That’s how we build a balanced and healthy future together.” And she’s not alone. Among the Republican caucus are six young legislators—Caldwell, Barron, Gonzalez, Johnson, Weinberg, and Soper—some already serving as House Whip and Minority Leader. In Washington, Lauren Boebert (CD-4) and Gabe Evans (CD-8) bring that same youthful energy to Congress. Add two young State Board of Education members and a CU Regent, and it’s clear: Colorado’s future is being shaped by conviction, not complacency. For a bit of history—Colorado’s youngest known legislator was Thomas W. Nevin, elected in 1934 at just 24 years, 8 months, and 26 days old—technically younger than the constitutional minimum at the time. Former Rep. Jerry Kopel and the Secretary of State’s archives confirmed Nevin’s early election, proving that youthful leadership in Colorado politics is nothing new—it’s part of our state’s DNA. These young leaders aren’t figureheads. They’re writing bills, fighting red tape, defending parental rights, and standing up for freedom. Groups like the , and are fueling that same fire at the grassroots—connecting new voters, training future leaders, and rebuilding from the ground up. Meanwhile, we've had an influx of young people submitting commentaries here at Rocky Mountain Voice. Two of this week's submissions among our most read: has been unafraid to take on the establishment. His reporting from Mesa County exposed how the National Education Association’s 2025 handbook prioritized ideology over education—until it was quietly deleted from public view. And argued that the true war we face today isn’t fought with bullets, but with ideas—and across Colorado, more people are waking up and choosing to fight for truth. Colorado’s youth aren’t buying into cynicism or apathy. They’re reclaiming their state—with truth, courage, and conviction. This movement isn’t just political. It’s spiritual. It’s cultural. It’s generational. Because when the next generation stands up—mountains move. In liberty and truth, Team RMV | | | | | TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK ** BY JEN SCHUMANN, The crowd inside Phil Long Music Hall stood and cheered as Victor Marx made it official. On Oct. 1, the Marine veteran and founder of All Things Possible Ministries announced his run for Colorado governor, joined by local pastors, veterans and elected officials—backing his call to restore justice and public safety. BY SHAINA COLE, A storm continues to brew at Vail Ski and Snowboard Academy (VSSA) after a longtime teacher’s controversial remarks in class. The comments came shortly after conservative activist Charlie Kirk was assassinated, setting off heated debate in the community about free speech, professional boundaries, and accountability in schools. BY JEN SCHUMANN, Durango School District 9-R holds its State of the District tonight at the Impact Career Innovation Center. The district’s event page bills it as a community update with test-score dashboards and a Q&A. The Durango Herald said leaders plan to confront “misinformation.” Parent Jason Mietchen hears it differently: “We’ve had to counteract the misinformation for years. The school puts out a ton of it.” | STATE BY MICHAEL HANCOCK, Colorado’s roads crumble, our classrooms overflow, and every budget cycle we hear the same refrain: “It’s TABOR’s fault.” Critics of the Taxpayer’s Bill of Rights insist that this constitutional guardrail has starved government of the resources it needs to educate children, maintain infrastructure, and keep the state running. But what if the potholes and crowded classrooms aren’t a symptom of too little revenue, but of misplaced priorities? The U.S. Department of Agriculture’s planned relocation of up to 2,600 employees to Fort Collins could bring more than $1 billion in new business output and over 6,000 new jobs to the area by the end of next year, according to a study by the think tank Common Sense Institute. BY MIKE O'DONNELL, A few weeks ago, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announced that it had inadvertently overstated—by more than 100%—the number of new jobs created across the United States during the last full year of the Biden/Harris Administration. | NATIONAL Several groups of cattle producers could be seen walking the halls of Congress and the U.S. Department of Agriculture in recent weeks. No, they weren’t lost. They were detailing some of the ways government prevents cattle producers from being profitable and identifying solutions that could help. Millions of dollars sent to Barack Obama’s foundation has ended up being donated to a progressive fund that supported anti-Israel groups, accused of setting up encampments at Ivy League universities, according to public documents. The federal government is officially entering a partial shutdown on Wednesday after the midnight funding deadline passed with Democrats and Republicans failing to agree on a funding bill. | LOCAL BY SHAINA COLE, Denver’s budget has grown sharply over the past ten years, far faster than its population and its ability to bring in tax dollars. A new analysis from the Common Sense Institute shows inflation-adjusted spending per resident rose about 60 percent since 2015. BY ANDY JONES, As the leaves turn in Douglas County, so does the page to another pivotal moment in our community’s story: the school board election on November 4, 2025. For parents juggling carpools, teachers fine-tuning lesson plans, and students eyeing college applications, this vote might feel like just another item on a crowded ballot. BY JEN SCHUMANN, Hunter Opilla didn’t expect to speak at a school board meeting when his family moved to Durango two years ago. But after learning about the district’s gender bathroom policy—and the board’s decision to reverse a superintendent directive—he says he felt he had no choice. | COMMENTARY BY C. J. GARBO, Picture, for a moment, a reversal of roles. Suppose a radical on the right assassinated one of the most visible voices on the left in front of live cameras. Imagine if, after that horror, crowds of conservatives cheered, called for more, and excused the violence as justified. BY LAUREEN BOLL, DCSD recently voted in favor of requiring parental consent, or “opt-in,” for students to participate in the upcoming Healthy Kids Colorado Survey, also known as HKCS. HKCS is an anonymous survey that is offered to all school districts in the state every-other-year, and much of the information that’s collected from middle and high school students is deeply personal, invasive, and has nothing to do with academics. BY STEVE HARRIS, Though I was not in attendance in 1922 (don’t laugh), the minutes of the negotiations are very thorough. At the first Commission meeting starting on January 30, 1922, the negotiations were centered on separating the Colorado River flow by the potential for irrigated lands in each state. BY DRAKE HUNTER, When I was younger, I was fascinated with Disneyland in Anaheim, California. My dad had worked there, and my mom had books on Walt Disney—how he dreamed up new worlds, turned them into concepts, and then made them a reality. | Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share? We'd love to talk with you about becoming a RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to mailto:tori@rockymountainvoice.com or send us a message on social media to chat! | 🚴♀️ DID YOU KNOW? Queen Victoria made the tricycle a royal trend. In 1881, while touring the Isle of Wight, Queen Victoria noticed a woman riding a tricycle and was instantly fascinated. She ordered two for herself and even requested that the inventor, James Starley, deliver them personally. Once the queen was seen riding, tricycles quickly became a must-have among Britain’s elite. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. As you may know, our focus at the California Democratic Party is grassroots organizing to pass Proposition 50.
This ballot measure was in direct response to Donald Trump’s election-rigging scheme — which means it came out of nowhere, with little time for us to plan or budget beforehand !
We have to organize, persuade undecided voters, and get out the vote faster than ever before. And so we need MASSIVE on-the-ground visibility.
That’s why we’re printing a new batch of signs for activists and volunteers to use at rallies, canvassing, and community events. These signs make it clear that YES on Prop 50 is our only chance at stopping Trump’s election-rigging scheme.
Will you take a look at our sign mockup below and consider pitching in a donation? They are flying off the shelves faster than expected! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-oct25-redistricting?&refcode=&amount=]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-oct25-redistricting?refcode=&amount= [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-oct25-redistricting?refcode=&amount=] CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-oct25-redistricting?&refcode=&amount=]Donald Trump and MAGA Republicans thought they had the perfect gerrymandering scheme to rig Congress in their favor before next year’s midterm elections even begin.
But, Prop 50 will stop Donald Trump’s election-rigging scheme dead in its tracks by counteracting MAGA Republican gerrymandering in Texas and other states.
But unlike red states, we are putting the power in the hands of the people to decide whether to temporarily change our Congressional maps. And that means a special election on November 4th — in less than a month.
Getting the word out about a special election in such a short timeframe means we need MASSIVE visibility and awareness with voters. And our new signs will be a huge part of that effort. The only problem? They aren’t cheap. We are printing these signs in multiple languages and are having trouble keeping them in stock.
Will you rush a donation right now to help us fund our outreach and visibility efforts? We want to make sure that our volunteers, organizers, and supporters have the resources they need at key events to get the word out about YES on Prop 50. Thank you! subject: Hello again!

body:
Can I get your best contact?| | | __Click here if you wish to unsubscribe__
---
Dear Peter, >From the bottom of my heart, I'd like to thank you for the honor and privilege of serving as your lieutenant governor for the people of our great state. Public service means everything to me. I LOVE traveling our state and hearing directly from Alaskans like you, absorbing your struggles and dreams alike. _It's the stories of hard work, growth, and grit that can only be found right here in Alaska that keep me going!_ **Alaska's greatness is worth fighting to preserve for future generations.** As a grandmother of 11, that mission is personal to me. I've always been sincere and direct with you, and you know I will never feed you cheap poll-tested lines that say a lot but mean nothing. _You are smarter than that._ For now, let me say this -**I hear you.** **_[Please take a moment to ensure I have the most up-to-date and direct information for you in my contacts.](http://links.mail.nancyforalaska.com/u/click?_t=070db738c7d848a188c7a9e8a29f1bde&_m=30f7500e039443cda4d1b3a43a842a2e&_e=7ghv878jZhVYOKrvU4jMLK0KIBU1uAeHyjaxUeOrTQdyCRWnPuP-sKjXIzr4mYOJnpx0AL51rDFwdA_z5rSIMmSYSk-tRvHjFf76FeP-o-a0oMxRobbS0z_Zt1rqI6Yd4IVRdDKv4b6O6Cc0UI-yMGfE_TLA3OWPiti8-eNuHhJRf3Nv9E8c6cGCMT_CeiAjbKBZWvPMLOvm06XFi4hCDHR5IZgI0z9mFWaSIC91ewF3YN1KF3Dh33FRjpX09wjAL3gNRZ7ntraSGyn0xbOLt9ZHJI3bWMoLAx8uuloXpoDKacn_FAg76vWA-2ybso_OFdQOZ-7S_OJy3LuUTWtvnA%3D%3D)_** Over the coming months, I would like you to be among the first to receive important updates on the issues that matter most—and some exciting announcements, too! _North to the Future._

clean_body: Can I get your best contact?| | | Click here if you wish to unsubscribe Dear Peter, >From the bottom of my heart, I'd like to thank you for the honor and privilege of serving as your lieutenant governor for the people of our great state. Public service means everything to me. I LOVE traveling our state and hearing directly from Alaskans like you, absorbing your struggles and dreams alike. It's the stories of hard work, growth, and grit that can only be found right here in Alaska that keep me going! Alaska's greatness is worth fighting to preserve for future generations. As a grandmother of 11, that mission is personal to me. I've always been sincere and direct with you, and you know I will never feed you cheap poll-tested lines that say a lot but mean nothing. You are smarter than that. For now, let me say this -I hear you. Over the coming months, I would like you to be among the first to receive important updates on the issues that matter most—and some exciting announcements, too! North to the Future. | | Nancy Dahlstrom P.S. Please take a moment to follow my social media channels so you don't miss even more from me! | | | ---|---|--- | | Paid for by Nancy Dahlstrom For Governor P.O.Box 770725,Eagle River, Alaska 99577

subject: Republicans Focused on Protecting our Communities, while Democrats take the Government Hostage

body:
No images? Click here https://indianarepublicancentralcommittee.cmail20.com/t/y-e-qhriiit-hhlljjcji-k/

Republicans Focused on Protecting our Communities, while Democrats Take the Government Hostage

Dear Hoosier Republicans,

This week, Governor Mike Braun showed once again why Indiana is leading the nation in law and order. From Fort Wayne to Lafayette to Indianapolis, he stood shoulder-to-shoulder with the Indiana State Police, thanking them for their tireless work to protect Hoosier families. Together, they are cracking down on drugs, protecting our children from predators, and making sure criminals who threaten our communities are behind bars.

But while Indiana is focused on protecting families, Washington Democrats are once again playing political games at the expense of hardworking Americans. Senator Schumer and his caucus shut down the federal government—not to protect our troops, veterans, or first responders—but to push free healthcare for illegal immigrants. That’s wrong. Republicans are standing strong with President Trump and Governor Braun to keep America safe, lower healthcare costs, and put Hoosiers first.

As always, thank you for being a part of our team, and please feel free to reach out if there's anything I can assist with.

Yours in service,

Lana Keesling, Indiana GOP Chairwoman

Governor Braun Celebrates State Police Successes to Keep Hoosier Communities Safe

Governor Mike Braun visited Indiana State Police posts in Allen, Tippecanoe, and Marion counties to thank troopers for their work protecting Hoosiers and to highlight major public safety wins. So far this year, ISP has seized large amounts of fentanyl, heroin, cocaine, and meth, made over 100 fentanyl-related arrests, and reduced drunk driving fatalities by nearly 12%.

“The State of Indiana is proud of our police officers, and we appreciate the work they’re doing to remove deadly fentanyl and illegal firearms from our streets, stop drunk driving fatalities, and put criminals who threaten Hoosiers’ lives in prison."

Governor Braun Spotlights Operation Guardian Angel Successes in Protecting Hoosier Kids

Governor Mike Braun announced that Indiana State Police’s Operation Guardian Angel has rescued 14 children from ongoing abuse since launching in August, while also leading to 48 arrests, including 14 hands-on offenders. The initiative, part of the Internet Crimes Against Children (ICAC) Task Force, focuses on proactive investigations and prevention efforts to protect kids online. As Cybersecurity Awareness Month begins, Braun urged parents to stay vigilant by monitoring their children’s online activity, setting boundaries, and using available safety resources at internetcrimesagainstkids.com [https://www.internetcrimesagainstkids.com].

"Nothing is more important than keeping Hoosier kids safe. I want to commend the Indiana State Police for their exceptional work on Operation Guardian Angel, bringing Indiana children to safety and those who harm them to justice." -Governor Mike Braun

📱 News You Can Use

Governor Mike Braun commemorated [https://x.com/GovBraun/status/1973862302798532795] the breaking of ground of FedEx's new 322,000 sq. ft. distribution facility in Gary.

Lieutenant Governor Micah Beckwith a [https://www.hoosieragtoday.com/2025/09/25/taiwan-braun-isda-icmc-isa/] nnounced [https://www.hoosieragtoday.com/2025/09/25/taiwan-braun-isda-icmc-isa/] that Taiwan signed two agreements with the Indiana Corn Marketing Council & the Indiana Soybean Alliance to purchase $6.4B of U.S. corn and soybeans over the next 4 years.

Secretary Diego Morales joined international and state leaders at the Taiwan Agricultural Trade Goodwill Mission signing ceremony, celebrating a long-standing partnership between Taiwan and Indiana.

Treasurer Daniel Elliott m [https://x.com/TOS_Elliott/status/1973095290983919738] et [https://x.com/TOS_Elliott/status/1973095290983919738] with local leaders from across the state at the Indiana Counties Annual Conference.

Comptroller Elise Nieshalla s [https://www.in.gov/comptroller/files/State-Comptroller-Nieshalla-Speaks-at-National-Debt-Crisis-Forum-at-the-U.S.-Capitol.pdf] poke [https://www.in.gov/comptroller/files/State-Comptroller-Nieshalla-Speaks-at-National-Debt-Crisis-Forum-at-the-U.S.-Capitol.pdf] at the National Debt Crisis Forum to discuss the need to rein in the national debt.

Attorney General Todd Rokita announced [https://events.in.gov/event/court-rules-indiana-may-require-biological-sex-on-birth-certificates-rather-than-retroactive-listings-of-gender-identity?utm_campaign=widget&utm_medium=widget&utm_source=State+of+Indiana] a federal district court affirmed Indiana law requiring biological sex, not "gender identity,"to be listed on birth certificates.

Senator Todd Young j [https://www.young.senate.gov/newsroom/press-releases/young-indiana-delegation-members-advocate-for-funding-for-rural-healthcare/] oined [https://www.young.senate.gov/newsroom/press-releases/young-indiana-delegation-members-advocate-for-funding-for-rural-healthcare/] Indiana's Congressional delegation in urging the HHS to allocate the highest possible level of funding from the newly established Rural Health Transformation Program to Indiana.

Senator Jim Banks introduced [https://www.banks.senate.gov/news/press-releases/sen-banks-pushes-to-secure-unique-zip-codes-for-indiana-communities/] the bipartisan Zip Code Bill. This bill would require the Postal Service to give certain communities in Indiana their own ZIP Codes, making mail delivery clearer and more efficient.

Congressman Rudy Yakym l [https://yakym.house.gov/posts/rep-yakym-leads-bipartisan-letter-to-improve-rural-healthcare-for-hoosiers] ed [https://yakym.house.gov/posts/rep-yakym-leads-bipartisan-letter-to-improve-rural-healthcare-for-hoosiers] with Hoosier veterans in DC with Honor Flight Northeast Indiana. The trip was made possible in part by the Walorski Family Foundation. Congressman Yakym praised former Congresswoman Jackie Walorski efforts to advocate for veterans stating, "Jackie was a tireless advocate for veterans, and I'm honored to carry on her legacy. We are a grateful nation."

Congressman Marlin Stutzman joined [https://x.com/RepStutzman/status/1970281406384513088] the Washington Reporter to discuss Charlie Kirk’s legacy and the latest going on in Washington.

Congressman Jim Baird c [https://x.com/RepJimBaird/status/1973503409170030661] ondemned [https://x.com/RepJimBaird/status/1973503409170030661] Democrats in Congress for holding "the government hostage to try to score political points and pass a $1.5 TRILLION radical wish list."

Congresswoman Victoria Spartz w [https://x.com/RepSpartz/status/1973766080209547345] ished [https://x.com/RepSpartz/status/1973766080209547345] Indiana's Jewish community a meaningful day of reflection and renewal.

Congressman Jefferson Shreve refused [https://shreve.house.gov/media/press-releases/shreve-refuse-salary-during-potential-government-shutdown-donate-johnson] to accept his Congressional salary for the duration of the government shutdown, instead donating it to the Johnson County Boys and Girls Club.

Congressman Mark Messmer joined [https://messmer.house.gov/news/documentsingle.aspx?DocumentID=187] legislation to ensure members of the U.S. Armed Forces, including the Coast Guard, will never miss a paycheck in the event of a government shutdown.

Congresswoman Erin Houchin introduced [https://houchin.house.gov/media/press-releases/houchin-auchincloss-introduce-first-house-bill-ai-chatbot-companions] the AI Warnings And Resources for Education (AWARE) Act, the first House legislation to address the growing use of AI chatbot companions by minors.

Comptroller Elise Nieshalla s [] poke [] at the National Debt Crisis Forum to discuss the need to rein in the national debt. Attorney General Todd Rokita announced [] a federal district court affirmed Indiana law requiring biological sex, not "gender identity,"to be listed on birth certificates. Senator Todd Young j [] oined [] Indiana's Congressional delegation in urging the HHS to allocate the highest possible level of funding from the newly established Rural Health Transformation Program to Indiana. Senator Jim Banks introduced [] the bipartisan Zip Code Bill. This bill would require the Postal Service to give certain communities in Indiana their own ZIP Codes, making mail delivery clearer and more efficient. Congressman Rudy Yakym l [] ed [] with Hoosier veterans in DC with Honor Flight Northeast Indiana. The trip was made possible in part by the Walorski Family Foundation. Congressman Yakym praised former Congresswoman Jackie Walorski efforts to advocate for veterans stating, "Jackie was a tireless advocate for veterans, and I'm honored to carry on her legacy. We are a grateful nation." Congressman Marlin Stutzman joined [] the Washington Reporter to discuss Charlie Kirk’s legacy and the latest going on in Washington. Congressman Jim Baird c [] ondemned [] Democrats in Congress for holding "the government hostage to try to score political points and pass a $1.5 TRILLION radical wish list." Congresswoman Victoria Spartz w [] ished [] Indiana's Jewish community a meaningful day of reflection and renewal. Congressman Jefferson Shreve refused [] to accept his Congressional salary for the duration of the government shutdown, instead donating it to the Johnson County Boys and Girls Club. Congressman Mark Messmer joined [] legislation to ensure members of the U.S. Armed Forces, including the Coast Guard, will never miss a paycheck in the event of a government shutdown. Congresswoman Erin Houchin introduced [] the AI Warnings And Resources for Education (AWARE) Act, the first House legislation to address the growing use of AI chatbot companions by minors. Upcoming Events October 4: Switzerland County Lincoln Day Dinner [] October 5: Red Bench Candidate School [] October 15: Howard County Elephant Stampede [] October 16: Monroe County Lincoln Day Dinner [] October 20: Knox County GOP Lincoln Day [] October 27: Indiana GOP 2025 Fall Dinner [] October 28: Madison County Fall Faith & Freedom Dinner [] October 28: Delaware County Lincoln Day Dinner [] October 29: DeKalb County Lincoln Day Dinner [] February 20: Congress of Counties 2026 [] March 7: Carroll County Lincoln Day Dinner [] June 19: Indiana Republican State Convention 2026 [] Click Here to Learn more about Governor Braun's Administrative Orders, Policy Proposals, and Leadership Appointments [] [] PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE. ## Trump Defies Court, Sends Guard to Oregon
President Trump on Sunday sent [300 California National Guard troops to Portland](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2u2zAQhE9DdTRWpH7eK1SkUee0KYW1dmkzoLkEubLh2wfKFV4xwAADfJiZP9ftijHFfP-NT140yJb53RKrct0eR6bK1LRiKUyS-cWVm743B260PViYNsKYPtvQ0RKYHfuOl34ev-dhBD90j4V7ID-eCvvOAxLg1_fty03e-f0GQxeXk9YDTD2AH_0lEN3m_jYjzoFx6s0ATFGlXv5KzPrgkD7y4nrZ5dml5aFamvG_jFuNW_OnSNMzMm49wcatPRi3wumOZs99xq1aj2exxCFys4GJKya7y1HVNs7UrAewO6YYpOaINqNGyZjs_cBKVqtIaVbFFqmaMJOVynfJxq1dXai8Y0qxTbMZ4P7EmP6X1R89_FrcvwAAAP__2KaRsA), Oregon, defying a federal court order that blocked him from deploying Oregon’s own Guard. California Gov. Gavin Newsom condemned the order as “un-American,” [vowing to sue](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHO4yAQhJ8Gd1gYDCQFxTXucu2V1hrW8Z4wIExi5e1PTn3dX22xM6P55s9jfgBFSs_fsKNra54TnkfE1rDO2yuFiuFoFUrBkBO-seLRzlkKqfkguDBzAIqfeeyCWxElqg7dYPXdjlqosduc92Lx6nb3CKD1YjzYRXnU4j7ezGKGjtyVNghhBiGUVv0awmKHxQLYFcEMbBQYqOXa_82U2oZr_OQ31t7nvYtua60cTP1icmJyOs-zT4u_KK4_k1PJkRr5g8mp1ddeOISdEl1UjXJicvIQac01EfDLl3f-PyFP3wORP19QAw9YYv7smBqvPoFU2krTVRfKSTHSYSwbxXMHit-i7Ufrvp38FwAA__8iiZST) and accusing Trump of using the military as “political pawns.” The statement came a day after U.S. District Judge Karin Immergut, who was appointed by Trump, [blocked](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2unDAUBOCnMZ1X_mMNhYs0dDdtSnTwOQbfeDEyZq19-4i8wq1HGs18f77mL4gp7utveJGrIc87tTNRrVTm7dqxEJ61wHEQ5p3eVOisbVZC9VwKLp4zQkyf2XToApEi3ZGTth-t6YU23eawN14DqGUI4yi9RyAvrBQozYABbBfd3SaFeEohdK8fAXGxcrEANhA8JTOCMNZcHt857nWjkD75TeXh86tLbqv1OJn-xdTE1NRae_xd250xNUGp0SdiarpvMTWl7CExNQVCKpD494Ur8XLdBDwXWvPOE7TzipXXkvNxcqQj5Q8hP3KpCXZkalKD5oGATL9orsziuRmD5gA4cDn0NHhLcrFjVxweLaYUz6dlRqwviOn_7voj7LdT_wIAAP__hTOVNg) the deployment of the Oregon National Guard to Portland. The temporary restraining order on troop mobilization expires Oct. 18, setting up a major legal and political fight over presidential power and the limits of military involvement at home.
## Hundreds Trapped in Snowstorm on Mt Everest
Hundreds of tourists hiking on the eastern slope of Mount Everest in Tibet were [trapped](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkM2OmzAURp_G7Iz8A7hZeEFVsUs2bdUlMtzr4MbYyHbCJE8_YjTzBLM-0qfvnH_n8Wycd-F6MSvqYuMYcM8eS8E0LvcACSGXZLYNIQZ8YMJc9lEw0VLOKOtGMM4_x6YCbREFygo1V-1JNS2TTbVow0QLHBuh5pNVzIoJgE-W_-Bto6SEyuljjTPWccZkK2sLMCk-KWOURdNx0jAEV2Kq_0cXyoLWP-MDUz3HtfJ6KWXLRPZEDEQM-77Xaw4HI2LAQO-ZiOGQOmBMHogYvsRotLQkvN0wZYp5NhtSm-JKJ-9eL5OA5pLu841-ilMXaHETFiIGk2jf88vf_u0PkUOcHRD56-dREvf8G9NWJQ3b7rx3uVOkYdfVOP_xuXwr9EOL9wAAAP__GxKVVQ) in a severe snowstorm over the weekend. Local operators said they had guided 350 trekkers to safety in a small township and were in contact with the remaining 200-plus still out in the snowy conditions. Hikers reported that their tents were collapsing under heavy snowfall and that “hypothermia was a real risk.” The storm, which locals said was [extraordinary](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkT2O2zAQRk9DdTRI6i9bqHCQqNttgiClMNZ8shhQJEHSFnT7QMbmBFu_wQPmfX_ep3eyzvr7B20YyhImjz07lII0rQ_PCZxLohjBweOJhFz2ySjTSq2k6iYm646pqXhYAIO6wqD79q1vWlU31Trw3DVvum3N3HZdt_C3ukG_sJm5JSKoyg6nTSvVaaXqtr4szLde33qifgF1WjQKbEtIl7_B-rJicUd4Il3msFVuWEuJWdRXYUZhxn3fL1v2JxNmhJePLMwYHzdnZ5lpQTkkeZbYkO7ws8XJV5Ar6wv8v3FI5UQelNwhtVRKyYgQHeRnEBm83MLDF_kZRmYX4ktISV6v-uO34Z-iHsNsWdQ_vp-dsedfSLFKA8fdOmdz14tG3Tey7vVR-dIMz8H8CwAA__9xNp_L) for October, hit at peak tourist season for the world’s tallest mountain. This weekend was particularly full as trekkers took advantage of an eight-day Chinese holiday. Early reports suggested [nearly 1,000 people](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHO3CAMwPGnIRsnMCT0BoYu2b6uHSMSzIWKQAS-RHn7KtX1Cb7NkqW__PPvr-nLxRTz65fb0FIoU8azJSTCOq3v7Cv6RtXtO_qS8cCKjc4JBPRcCi6GybuYrkl33gZEQNWhlaZ_Gt0LpbvVznKRoDT8cICgtAGjwizcUy_CPz1CF-1dk0IMUgjVq0fwfjZyNs6ZgG6QTAv0kUp9_Ckx04ohXeXA-ljK1iW7Eu2NqZ8MRgZjvvbS6F4xGO8wg1EKBqO4p7PU5PlNZDD-5_GPj5fMN-IfJJ8vvrnW4oG8Uakbd417bDtWR8grtuWNHEMolXhy77yseFe7av1-xpRiGwzT4rW5mP6dSt_672HhbwAAAP__pViRQw) were trapped in the severe weather, but it was unclear whether trekking guides and staff were accounted for or whether the north face of Everest, also in Tibet, was affected.
## Best Advent Calendars for 2025
Let the countdown to the countdown begin! Advent calendars are already starting to sell out—including a [giant Lindt version spotted at Costco](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1ugzAUxfGngc3IX-Bk8NAlW7p2RBffS3Dl2Mi-BeXtq_QVOp-fdKT_132-Q0wxPz7hSZ7XMmc6WyJmqvP2k7ESNq6w74Ql00GVGp-zlnoUSgo5zQgxvWbbo1-JNJmevHLj1dlRGttvHkktbz65YMJChgJO0lq8SnTa2NBH_56VlJOS0oxmWBEXpxYH4FaCSXVWEkYudfguMfNGa3qVg-oQyrNPfmPeW2c-On3r9O08zwFSqhTiTu1NOn0LpXEoIsWMLB4RMgvAgzKLAIkyQhVKXbS-SNVXj_sZU4ptcp2VjyfE9PfE_8pzeP0bAAD__-1Ses4)—so even with 80 days until Christmas, there are only 55 until December 1, when most calendars start unveiling their surprises. If you’re shopping for younger fans, [kid-focused Advent calendars](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDHO4yAYRE-DOyzANiQFxTbusu2W1ucwxOwSsIA4yu1XzhX-bqSRnmben9tyoxBDevymJ2zzeUl414jWUJbtlVyBq63QvsPlhAMFtb0XJdTEpeBCL45C_Cxj56wHFIYOVprpasZJDGO3Wee1uhtHk5aXVUt_mWA8jVeCEaseZRfsSZNCaCnEMA29d241cjVExoO0ZKOACy2X_m8OqW3w8ZMPlP6en120W2t7ZcMvpmamZooRVGqf0JiaTzJTsxRMzeJMbQNfURsndyA1fqeI5KhUnj3_vqICvqGAU3KcGi-h_jvLinh64vl1grti3f4OMYaqDRvF40khfge1H1k8rPofAAD__8hyhe4) include LEGO’s Minecraft and Star Wars sets, Bluey book bundles, Squishmallows collections, and Magna-Tiles’ new microMAGS calendar. If you want to go fancy with your Christmas countdown, _Town & Country_ has you covered with its choices for the [38 best luxury Advent calendars of 2025](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2unTAQBOCnMR3IP4APhYs0dDdtSrSXXYMjYyN7L4i3j855hdTzaUaaP1_LF4QY0vYbDnLs85LorpGYqSz7T8JCWLnAeRLmRBcVqnwvWuqhVbKV44IQ4rP0DTpPpMk05JQdJtsP0vTN7gheaPXLkjGrkuNrHVGPk_LWT8oMHprg3m1KylFJaQbTecRvq74tgPUEoxK9JAycS_c3h8Q7-fjki0q35qOJbmc-qzC_hJ6Fnu_77jjfCRKu-SdxeQ7Y3lLoufITSejZQ91DTi0XSliFnjc9WfkJ0vq0gBclbleIlBDKGzTF4XmHGEMdrejldkCIn33-r9Mup_8FAAD__8yGgt0), ranging from Anthropologie’s 24 Days of Beauty to Jo Malone colognes to Flaviar’s whiskey calendar. _Southern Living’s_ list of the 42 best Advent calendars shares a few notable options with _Town & Country_, but also includes [some more affordable choices](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1uwyAUQOGnsTdb_NMMDF2ypWtHC3KvYyoMFtwY5e2r9BW6H33D-b4tNx9TzI8vv6OjtSwZe0tIhHXZnhkqQqPqjwOhZDyxYqO-CCb0xNnEzAI-pteiRnArokA5ouNWX6zSTKpxc6isDYFzxYW56Lsx0upVggmBSRQYxujeGmfMcMaklvMKECwP1nu7ojd8UAwhUqnzT4mZNlzTq5xY53vZx-Q2oqMN8nMQ10Fce-9zK0_asOYUz5gf72wQ14CNJg8nZpruPmEGX9v0IY3UWozVwdFjSrEZOyj22H1Mfz79a8rpxG8AAAD___zOdz0). This list includes McCrea’s caramel candies and a 12-day hot sauce sampler from Uncommon Goods.

**➤****President Trump** spoke Sunday to thousands of sailors at Naval Station Norfolk, marking the Navy’s 250th anniversary with a patriotic address focused on military strength and investment. Joined by First Lady Melania Trump and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, Trump praised the Navy’s history and vowed continued support for its global dominance. On this day in 1927, The Jazz Singer premiered, widely celebrated as the first feature-length “talkie” with synchronized dialogue. . California Gov. Gavin Newsom condemned the order as “un-American,” and accusing Trump of using the military as “political pawns.” The statement came a day after U.S. District Judge Karin Immergut, who was appointed by Trump, the deployment of the Oregon National Guard to Portland. The temporary restraining order on troop mobilization expires Oct. 18, setting up a major legal and political fight over presidential power and the limits of military involvement at home. Hundreds Trapped in Snowstorm on Mt Everest Hundreds of tourists hiking on the eastern slope of Mount Everest in Tibet were in a severe snowstorm over the weekend. Local operators said they had guided 350 trekkers to safety in a small township and were in contact with the remaining 200-plus still out in the snowy conditions. Hikers reported that their tents were collapsing under heavy snowfall and that “hypothermia was a real risk.” The storm, which locals said was for October, hit at peak tourist season for the world’s tallest mountain. This weekend was particularly full as trekkers took advantage of an eight-day Chinese holiday. Early reports suggested were trapped in the severe weather, but it was unclear whether trekking guides and staff were accounted for or whether the north face of Everest, also in Tibet, was affected. name: Getting gory
subject: MAGA escalations reach new low in 2025 Pennsylvania Court Race
body:
MAGA’s lies and deceptions have been completely unleashed on this statewide race in Pennsylvania that could have huge implications for 2028
| | |
---
| | [](https://wep8ryhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001fXbhiXkYA-Wf7pilnQ37RcEqbKvD9MUfS3sjm1ZIJUforWDReW7kSlka4sXW9bKXCv0u33jCXyribE6IJMDI4fMAvwm29yD7aOdj-JMfKjV8f-e1wJzB6dUcmXUecogkxGRgJBSDy7bDV1KHJhc4NxQemmEz4tOMuE2ykszKZDgWhU7X6_lThQD59anSSXA4klOZL72HmKDuQPY4QSApa21Z4m0-fCW2jl_ox1i7E0E=&c=seKRF0rYgbB_mMTmr6KcBcgefs3XVPI17nT87R4Zuo1vpiOIqwSFAg==&ch=WhioO96uc71atlYDnj03-HlkwxFFJY0eKluqeHj8VJ4r8N101BoQSA==)
---
| Friend,
On November 4, Pennsylvanians will decide, in a simple yes or no vote, whether to retain three Democratic justices on the state Supreme Court. Democrats currently have a 5-2 majority.
While this may sound just like another provincial election, it's anything but. Whoever controls this court controls the penultimate swing state of Pennsylvania. MAGA hopes to seize the court so they can rig the 2026 midterms and 2028 Presidential.
There's an ugliness underlying this race: MAGA activists are lying to voters during this critical period as mail-in ballots are going out, deceiving them into believing that a 'no' vote would protect women's rights and establish term limits.
None of that is true.
---
| | [](https://wep8ryhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001fXbhiXkYA-Wf7pilnQ37RcEqbKvD9MUfS3sjm1ZIJUforWDReW7kSlka4sXW9bKXCv0u33jCXyribE6IJMDI4fMAvwm29yD7aOdj-JMfKjV8f-e1wJzB6dUcmXUecogkxGRgJBSDy7bDV1KHJhc4NxQemmEz4tOMuE2ykszKZDgWhU7X6_lThQD59anSSXA4klOZL72HmKDuQPY4QSApa2ckO0FGC-Raz5mjJma21KY=&c=seKRF0rYgbB_mMTmr6KcBcgefs3XVPI17nT87R4Zuo1vpiOIqwSFAg==&ch=WhioO96uc71atlYDnj03-HlkwxFFJY0eKluqeHj8VJ4r8N101BoQSA==)
---
| In response, we are left with two choices:
1) File formal complaints with the state elections board, local and national media, the FEC, or even the FCC--the same Trump-led agency that strongarmed Disney into pulling Jimmy Kimmel from the air temporarily (you can guess how our complaint would be received...straight into the trash bin).
or 2) we can grit our teeth, bare down, and fight back twice as hard, boxing out these galling lies with a more compelling story about what's at stake: our democracy and our freedoms.
We're choosing option #2. We're choosing to fight.
Trump's team wants to take this prize now to try and show that they aren't as weak as we know they actually are. And they want to capture this court for the longer term objective of keeping Trump in power--or at a minimum, preventing Democrats from ever winning PA again in a presidential election.
MAGA activists are doing and saying anything to win and they expect us to get frustrated, to give up, to get tired of the fight and give in. Well, we won't stop--as long as you are still in it with us.
[We need your help to shut down these lies and get our voters out in force between now and Election Day on November 4. subject: MAGA escalations reach new low in 2025 Pennsylvania Court Race

body:
MAGA’s lies and deceptions have been completely unleashed on this statewide race in Pennsylvania that could have huge implications for 2028
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0018qJw9ohF7amtnhSw89cqz4HCPhlHHWFpy_3ZrH2wT9xNl5gjAq16n2bn_Psl0-dpKb8IUE0EbdFWWqKK3cm8kr_9lT0YqKtht-vYoG6OkK2AW1gGc2j81mEN_Oahoe2QHAiGLwaoo4Y39cL9iWxkYZbbAjZwTQp9WYiXqRvMZcaA2o2Sx8Vb1wx1o8ZmYs90zV4b13B_Zm73OR8p6VjkmdbNs0BJmGwC&c=Dawl1fLtRigKtHpfE1Tda1MI3v7uqmBbeDcPeqYhTa8knCax7iU5AQ==&ch=JeFYGcYjCoaqZUdnptGP7222zOA2bCNrp2_SgabRz51HsOB51dbk1A==)
---
| Peter,
On November 4, Pennsylvanians will decide, in a simple yes or no vote, whether to retain three Democratic justices on the state Supreme Court. Democrats currently have a 5-2 majority.
While this may sound just like another provincial election, it's anything but. Whoever controls this court controls the penultimate swing state of Pennsylvania. MAGA hopes to seize the court so they can rig the 2026 midterms and 2028 Presidential.
There's an ugliness underlying this race: MAGA activists are lying to voters during this critical period as mail-in ballots are going out, deceiving them into believing that a 'no' vote would protect women's rights and establish term limits.
None of that is true.
---
| | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0018qJw9ohF7amtnhSw89cqz4HCPhlHHWFpy_3ZrH2wT9xNl5gjAq16n2bn_Psl0-dpKb8IUE0EbdFWWqKK3cm8kr_9lT0YqKtht-vYoG6OkK2AW1gGc2j81mEN_Oahoe2QHAiGLwaoo4Y39cL9iWxkYZbbAjZwTQp9WYiXqRvMZcaA2o2Sx8Vb1wx1o8ZmYs90zV4b13B_Zm73OR8p6VjkmYxl3m9R1tEP&c=Dawl1fLtRigKtHpfE1Tda1MI3v7uqmBbeDcPeqYhTa8knCax7iU5AQ==&ch=JeFYGcYjCoaqZUdnptGP7222zOA2bCNrp2_SgabRz51HsOB51dbk1A==)
---
In response, we are left with two choices:
1) File formal complaints with the state elections board, local and national media, the FEC, or even the FCC--the same Trump-led agency that strongarmed Disney into pulling Jimmy Kimmel from the air temporarily (you can guess how our complaint would be received...straight into the trash bin).
or 2) we can grit our teeth, bare down, and fight back twice as hard, boxing out these galling lies with a more compelling story about what's at stake: our democracy and our freedoms.
We're choosing option #2. We're choosing to fight.
Trump's team wants to take this prize now to try and show that they aren't as weak as we know they actually are. And they want to capture this court for the longer term objective of keeping Trump in power--or at a minimum, preventing Democrats from ever winning PA again in a presidential election.
MAGA activists are doing and saying anything to win and they expect us to get frustrated, to give up, to get tired of the fight and give in. Well, we won't stop--as long as you are still in it with us.
[We need your help to shut down these lies and get our voters out in force between now and Election Day on November 4. Can you pitch in $50, $25, or even $5 to help us counter the lies and deceptions and save the PA supreme court Dem majority?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0018qJw9ohF7amtnhSw89cqz4HCPhlHHWFpy_3ZrH2wT9xNl5gjAq16n2bn_Psl0-dpmhQxjE8mYvN4D5K9-4N6IDw3Ct4xpIQUK6i7N6IPPINGqtiYfVGlp-Ef4vxObhCetDi2fOBki3MYJWBvIm9_gxqMN-HgpI8s-RETw8NiR5ECiDWznc6QQKntIpEYOxNeon1JIshwrrXmJmTiQX0EEGvmTMrS9MtcJPgT3mjm1IEh1JmW8loS0g==&c=Dawl1fLtRigKtHpfE1Tda1MI3v7uqmBbeDcPeqYhTa8knCax7iU5AQ==&ch=JeFYGcYjCoaqZUdnptGP7222zOA2bCNrp2_SgabRz51HsOB51dbk1A==)

name: The Texas Flyover
subject: McAllen Official Charged, SA Brahmas Axed, and Most Bilingual Cities
body:
McAllen City Commissioner Rodolfo "Rudy" Castillo and his wife have been charged with money laundering, smuggling merchandise, and employing illegal immigrants at their used clothing business. Federal authorities [raided their home and business](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL-O8yAQxJ8Gd0QL_l9QfI27fO2V1hoWeyVsIuCS-O1PvjzC1TOamd983ec7cuBj_Y87mfL2cT7olQOVQmneLYZAR_SeLWOwG6aVXMZl1qBbqUBCNzvkcM5N5cwCblQVGdW3Y98MLdTVZhw60AR9jai1cgrsMmALvSKvWnBjxeYKUwCdUqDq8TZ02DdqqDUMQNCAaIAcl5huZaNCb8w-nPFJ6WbjXgWzlfLIov4n9CT0tJ9pfV6K0FOIFoO8gISerhahJwVCT1Bfzg-dtHHfOWeOByX5Yk_So-VjlZ4cJQxySbxQOuWHP0s-ZIoPlN8ZHUqLmYSeqmTc48UhcO560cC6I4ffheVvvz6N_gkAAP__bUKLww) on Thursday, and the couple surrendered on Friday.

| | |
---
| [](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OnTAQhZ_GdKDxD4ZbuIi0ut2mTWnNxQOMZAOyDbv79hGbKC-Q9pxPM0ffr3f_jhx5W35iIlc_591v9FEi1UrZpwljpG2fZ54Y47RiXigUfHkFqm8ltGB9QI5f3jTBvSA8ZENODv1jMGMPulmdgdGMOKGlEGRvJM12kL3U0CMo9dINu_uYBLBSgtSPbrQ4GDlqBSMQGBAGKHDdc1dXqvSJZY5f-0W5m_bURLfWehShfwj1FOrJ20WldoEvXrDcyJ3r51mTL_uZJxL6jRJyFMreYaLAZxL67UAO7YG5bpTLysfffsJ0IC_bN_GvlCO0Bgb_x8G5cUWvlVLWDlbbvskuHB8cIxc7CAPL_fB7b_0_y5dTvwMAAP__D6yPjg) | |
# Monday, October 6, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1839, Ben McCulloch—a Texas Ranger, U.S. marshal, and Confederate general—was [wounded in a duel](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGurCAQxvGnwU4zA6hYUNzGbm97SjPCqCQoGyTr-vYnu69wqn_1_Yrv5zE9KMRwrP9pZ1veS5oOvs7IpXCedkcx8pGWJbhA0W2UV_YnzZME2dYINXSTpxDvSVfezuAHrNhi3w69Ni2oarMzIioE9D21Ri0G3cIDuw5cq7rBqCrYD4YAHSKgGhrTUa_RKAkGGDQIDexDSbkpGxd-07nEO704Ny7tVbRbKc9TqH9CjkKO13U15dwoHTEc3KS8Cjl-V7Wnu57vT4Qc-Sj5U611la1_XiHGcHa90LDuFOIXL3-75GXlbwAAAP__RFRzzA) with a friend of Alonzo B. Sweitzer, whom he had recently defeated in an election to the Texas House of Representatives. His right arm was permanently crippled by the injury.
